text: Hemingway's morning routine in Cuba began before sunrise: he rose at first light, stood at a bookcase-top desk, and worked standing until his legs tired or the day's quota was met. The standing position was functional, not aesthetic — it kept him alert and prevented the drowsiness that accompanies sedentary work. His word counts, logged daily, rarely exceeded 500 words. He was not racing toward volume; he was protecting quality by stopping before exhaustion degraded judgment. The ritual imposed limits as deliberately as it imposed effort, because Hemingway understood that overworking a session poisons the next one.
question: According to the passage, why did Hemingway log his daily word counts?

options.text: To compete against his own previous records and build momentum
To track productivity for his publisher's contractual requirements
As part of a ritual that served both accountability and momentum
To prove to critics that disciplined output was possible without an outline
